mission.title: consul: Allow setting of SecretID and AccessorID on ACL tokens on Consul 1.4+
mission.description: Allow setting of SecretID and AccessorID on ACL tokens on Consul 1.4+. Requested by 14+ users.

steps.title: Check current consul deployment
steps.description:
Verify your consul version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
helm list -n consul 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working consul installation.

steps.title: Review consul configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant consul configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get configmap -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=consul
```
#### Feature Description

Pre v1.4 the token value of an ACL token could be specified by the client.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow setting of SecretID and AccessorID on ACL tokens on…
steps.description:
This PR allows for the ACL token accessor and secret ids, and the ACL policy id to be set to a user-supplied UUID during their first creation. These IDs are still immutable once set but this allows for external configuration management systems to generate the UUIDs and populate consul agent configurations without having to have yet spun up the consul servers to create the tokens.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n consul -l app.kubernetes.io/name=consul
kubectl get events -n consul --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow setting of SecretID and AccessorID on ACL tokens on…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This PR allows for the ACL token accessor and secret ids, and the ACL policy id to be set to a user-supplied UUID during their first creation. These IDs are still immutable once set but this allows for external configuration management systems to generate the UUIDs and populate consul agent configurations without having to have yet spun up the consul servers to create the tokens.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with consul installed or the issue environment reproducible.
